channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: House Votes to Extend Expiring Law on Warrantless Surveillance for 10 Days
item.description: The Senate would need to also approve the stopgap measure that passed the House early Friday. Libertarian-leaning House Republicans had balked at a long-term extension.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: Wiretapping and Other Eavesdropping Devices and Methods
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Gen. Joshua M. Rudd, the director of the National Security Agency, during his confirmation hearing in January.

item.title: Acting ICE Director Says He Plans to Resign in May
item.description: Todd Lyons said he would leave to spend more time with his family. He has spoken about a surge in threats against ICE officers, saying that he knew the reality firsthand.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo and Hamed Aleaziz
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The exit of Todd Lyons as acting director of Immigration and Customs Enforcement raises more questions about the direction of the agency, which has faced dimming public opinion.

item.title: Trump to Nominate Dr. Erica Schwartz, a Vaccine Supporter, as CDC Director
item.description: Dr. Erica Schwartz is seen as a highly qualified traditional choice and tapping her is the strongest signal yet that the administration is veering away from vaccine skepticism this election year.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
item.media:description: Dr. Erica G. Schwartz is a Navy officer and a retired rear admiral in the Commissioned Corps of the U.S. Public Health Service and a former deputy surgeon general.

item.title: Trump to Pick Ousted FEMA Head to Lead Agency Again
item.description: Cameron Hamilton is a former Navy SEAL who ran unsuccessfully for Congress.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, Scott Dance and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.media:credit: Jose Luis Magana/Associated Press
item.media:description: Cameron Hamilton told a House committee last May, when he was the acting chief of FEMA, that the agency was vital to communities “in their greatest times of need” and should not be eliminated. He was ousted the next day.

item.title: RFK Jr. Shifts Tone on Vaccines in Congressional Hearing
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr., who has sought to roll back longstanding vaccine policy, testified that the measles vaccine is safe and effective “for most people” and agreed it was safer than getting measles.
item.dc:creator: Sheryl Gay Stolberg and Dani Blum
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Democrats assailed Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. over his past statements on health policy.

item.title: D4vd Arrested in Connection With Death of Celeste Rivas Hernandez
item.description: The singer was detained in connection with the death of Celeste Rivas Hernandez, who died before her 15th birthday. D4vd’s lawyers said they would vigorously defend his innocence.
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens and Orlando Mayorquín
item.media:credit: Jordan Strauss/Invision, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The body of a teenage girl was found in a car belonging to the singer D4vd.

item.title: Who Are D4vd and Celeste Rivas Hernandez?
item.description: The musician is known for creating the anthem for Fortnite. He was touring for his major-label debut when Ms. Rivas Hernandez’s remains were found in his car, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens
item.media:credit: Pascal Le Segretain/Getty Images
item.media:description: The body of a girl was found in the front trunk of a Tesla belonging to the musician D4vd.

item.title: Roger Adams Dies at 71; Invented the Rolling Sneakers Known as Heelys
item.description: You could walk in them like gym shoes, but if you rocked back on your heels the wheels emerged, turning them into roller skates. In the early 2000s, the company sold millions.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir

item.title: Analilia Mejia, a Progressive Democrat, Wins Mikie Sherrill’s House Seat
item.description: Ms. Mejia, who helped run Sen. Bernie Sanders’s presidential campaign, beat her Republican opponent, Joe Hathaway, to win a seat Ms. Sherrill vacated after she was elected governor of New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully
item.media:credit: Bryan Anselm for The New York Times
item.media:description: Analilia Mejia, a progressive Democrat, will serve out the remaining eight months of Gov. Mikie Sherrill’s term in Congress.

item.title: Mamdani’s Tax Return: $1,600 From Rapping and $131,000 From Politics
item.description: In their 2025 joint tax return, Mayor Zohran Mamdani and his wife, Rama Duwaji, reported a combined income of roughly $145,000, including about $10,000 that she earned from art work.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rubinstein and Sally Goldenberg
item.media:credit: Shuran Huang for The New York Times
item.media:description: Most of the income reported by Zohran Mamdani and his wife, Rama Duwaji, came from his salary as a state assemblyman.

item.title: Mamdani Says He Plans to Skip the 2026 Met Gala
item.description: In an interview with the local news outfit Hell Gate, the New York mayor framed his decision to avoid the glamorous fund-raiser as a way to keep his focus on affordability.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani might have seemed out of place at the Met Gala, an event that is widely considered to be the playground of the rich and famous.

item.title: ICE Has Detained Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé, an 85-Year-Old Widow
item.description: After Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé’s husband died, an inheritance battle exploded. Her stepson then used his influence to have her arrested, an Alabama probate judge said.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Bogel-Burroughs and Catherine Porter

item.title: What We Know About Clavicular’s Apparent Overdose and Hospitalization
item.description: A harrowing incident involving Clavicular, ambassador to the “looksmaxxing” community, was captured on the same platforms that made him a star.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Bernstein
item.media:credit: Cassidy Araiza for The New York Times
item.media:description: Braden Peters, better known as Clavicular, was carried out of a bar in Miami this week after an apparent overdose. Not long after, he was back out on the town for a club event.

item.title: NPR Receives $113 Million From 2 Gifts
item.description: The donations, from the philanthropist Connie Ballmer and an anonymous donor, will support the network’s long-term strategy.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin
item.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times

item.title: Reed Hastings Will Leave Netflix as Board Chairman
item.description: The co-founder of the streaming giant will leave its board in June, the company said.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin
item.media:credit: Alex Goodlett for The New York Times
item.media:description: “My real contribution at Netflix wasn’t a single decision; it was a focus on member joy,” the Netflix co-founder and chairman Reed Hastings said in a letter about his coming departure.

item.title: Can Webcams Help Solve New York’s Restaurant Line Problem?
item.description: With carefully positioned cameras and user input, the website Damn Lines hopes to address the worst part of visiting popular restaurants.
item.dc:creator: T.M. Brown
item.category: John's of Bleecker Street (Manhattan, NY, Restaurant)
item.media:credit: Jake Borden for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lines at the West Village location of L’Industrie Pizzeria can stretch down the block.

item.title: Where Has All of New York City’s Outdoor Dining Gone?
item.description: The number of eateries with permits for sidewalk and roadway tables has dwindled to about a third of its pandemic-era peak.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy
item.media:credit: Sara Naomi Lewkowicz for The New York Times
item.media:description: Outdoor seating at Serafina in the meatpacking district in Manhattan, which has maintained its outdoor seating as many restaurants have abandoned Covid-era outdoor hutches.

item.title: Michigan’s Dam Crisis Shows Decay of U.S. Infrastructure, Whitmer Says
item.description: Workers in Cheboygan hurried to shore up a dam in danger of being overtopped, part of a “slow-moving disaster” threatening communities across the state.
item.dc:creator: Billy Witz, Sam Easter and Amy Graff
item.media:credit: Jan-Michael Stump/Traverse City Record-Eagle, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A team of municipality workers cleared debris from the Boardman River Weir that was blocking the flow of the flooded Boardman/Ottaway River in downtown Traverse City, Mich., on Wednesday.

item.title: A Paris Court Just Rewrote the Rules of Corporate Morality
item.description: The profit motive was on trial. The verdict was scathing.
item.dc:creator: M. Gessen
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)
item.media:credit: Photo illustration by The New York Times; photographs by Behrouz Mehri and Delil Souleiman/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Bruno Lafont, the former chief executive of Lafarge.

item.title: Trump Is the End of a 100-Year Experiment
item.description: A conservative court watcher explains why the president has failed to bend the judicial branch to his will.
item.dc:creator: Ross Douthat and Sophia Alvarez Boyd
item.media:credit: Saul Loeb/AFP via Getty Images

item.title: Stephen Colbert Delights in Trump’s ‘Ongoing Papal Feud’
item.description: The host of “The Late Show” recapped what he called “yet another day when the entire world is on edge over President Trump’s senseless and elective war — with the pope.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: An Arrest in the Streets of New York, on Horseback
item.description: An officer from the Police Department’s Mounted Unit raced through the Upper West Side of Manhattan, chasing a woman accused of snatching a purse.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack

item.title: Mandelson, a Friend of Epstein’s, Became U.S. Ambassador Despite Failing Security Vetting
item.description: Britain’s foreign office overruled vetting officials in granting Peter Mandelson, a friend of Jeffrey Epstein, the highest level of security clearance, the government said.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Carl Court
item.media:description: Peter Mandelson, then Britain’s ambassador to the United States, at the ambassador’s residence in Washington, in February last year.

item.title: Former Navy Service Member Charged in Georgia Killing Spree
item.description: The suspect is accused of carrying out a string of attacks in the Atlanta area that killed two women and injured a homeless man. The authorities described the attacks as random.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra
item.media:credit: Ben Hendren/Atlanta Journal-Constitution, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The street where Lauren Bullis, a Department of Homeland Security employee, was killed while walking her dog on Monday in Decatur, Ga.
